growth_records.medium: BG11_0 (nitrogen-free), nutrients 5x concentrated for HD batch

up.units: mg-Fe/L consumed over 7 d (from C_Fe 8.32->0.21 mg/L; >90% removed within first 6 h, biosorption-dominated)
not quantified as a rate; supplied as 1.5% CO2-enriched air + 250 mg/L NaHCO3
diazotrophic fixation, not quantified as a rate

sec.units: strongly increased under Fe limitation (<0.5 mg-Fe/L); absolute batch rate not tabulated

product_yields.note: no growth yield on a defined C substrate reported (photoautotrophic)

growth_records.cite: Lucato, Veronica et al. Iron accumulation dynamics in Nostoc sp. PCC 7120: extent of biosorption in batch and continuous reactors. Biotechnology for Biofuels and Bioproducts (2026). DOI:10.1186/s13068-026-02743-9. [PMC12931063]
growth_records.snippet: iron availability did not markedly influence the specific growth rate after 2 days of iron deprivation, with values ranging from 0.73 +/- 0.07 d-1 to 1.00 +/- 0.02 d-1 (Table S1)... sustained growth even in the absence of any iron supply...
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (diazotrophic cyanobacterium). Batch HD, 2-day Fe-deprivation pretreatment. Reported mu range 0.73-1.00 d-1 across C_Fe,0 = 0, 0.45, 0.80, 2.00, 4.50, 8.50 mg-Fe/L (no clear trend). Record uses the upper mu=1.00 d-1; per-condition mu values are in Table S1 (supplementary, not in fulltext). Conversions: growth_rate_per_h = 1.00 d-1 / 24 = 0.0417 h-1; doubling_time_h = ln2/1.00 d-1 = 0.693 d = 16.6 h (lower-bound mu 0.73 d-1 -> 0.0304 h-1, td 22.8 h). mu measured from slope of ln(OD750) vs time in exponential phase (growth_method=reported). Iron uptake arithmetic (C_Fe,0=8.32 mg/L condition, Table 1): 8.32 (t=0) - 0.21 (t=7 d) = 8.11 mg-Fe/L depleted; note ~1.74 mg/L already at t=6 h so removal is fast biosorption + intracellular uptake mixed, ICP-MS measured. Biomass 0.62->6.16 gX/L over run. Iron quota q_Fe reached up to 10,888 +/- 1256 mg-Fe/kgX at t=6 h then declined by growth dilution.

growth_records.medium: BG11_0 (nitrogen-free), nutrients 5x concentrated for HD batch

up.units: Fe-limited; growth rose with C_Fe,0 up to ~5 mg/L then saturated; Monod half-saturation constant k_Fe ~0.5-0.8 mg-Fe/L (fitted)
up.method: ICP-MS residual medium + Monod fit of mu vs C_Fe,0

sec.units: markedly increased under strong Fe limitation (<0.5 mg-Fe/L)

growth_records.cite: Lucato, Veronica et al. Iron accumulation dynamics in Nostoc sp. PCC 7120: extent of biosorption in batch and continuous reactors. Biotechnology for Biofuels and Bioproducts (2026). DOI:10.1186/s13068-026-02743-9. [PMC12931063]
growth_records.snippet: the 10-day iron deprivation pretreatment revealed a distinct relationship: the growth rate increased rapidly with increasing C_Fe,0 until reaching a threshold, beyond which further increases in iron availability no longer increased the grow
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote, batch HD, 10-day Fe-deprivation pretreatment => Fe-limited Monod growth. mu increased with C_Fe,0 (0, 0.3, 0.7, 1.3, 5.0, 12.0 mg-Fe/L), saturating above 5 mg/L at mu_max approaching the ~1.0 d-1 plateau seen in the 2-day set; k_Fe ~0.5-0.8 mg-Fe/L. growth_rate_per_h uses saturated mu_max ~1.00 d-1 /24 = 0.0417 h-1; doubling_time_h = ln2/1.00 = 16.6 h. Explicit per-condition mu values in Table S1 (supplementary, not in fulltext). mu from ln(OD750) slope (growth_method=reported). At low/no Fe the exponential phase was nearly absent.

up.units: steady-state consumption from C_Fe,in - C_Fe,out; internal quota q_Fe,i plus surface-adsorbed q_Fe,e both detectable; C_X saturated above C_Fe,in ~1.3 mg/L
up.method: ICP-MS of inlet vs outlet-filtered medium + EDTA-wash for adsorbed fraction

sec.units: mg/L (steady-state standing concentration at tau=1.2 d; nearly doubled vs 2.2 d)

growth_records.cite: Lucato, Veronica et al. Iron accumulation dynamics in Nostoc sp. PCC 7120: extent of biosorption in batch and continuous reactors. Biotechnology for Biofuels and Bioproducts (2026). DOI:10.1186/s13068-026-02743-9. [PMC12931063]
growth_records.snippet: as the inverse of the residence time in continuous reactors... residence times of 1.2 +/- 0.1 d and 2.2 +/- 0.1 d... At a residence time of 1.2 +/- 0.1 d, production nearly doubled to 239.6 +/- 37.7 mg... C_X increased proportionally to iro
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote chemostat (CSTR). At steady state mu = D = 1/tau. tau = 1.2 +/- 0.1 d => D = 1/1.2 = 0.833 d-1 = 0.833/24 = 0.0347 h-1; dilution_rate_per_h = growth_rate_per_h = 0.0347. doubling_time_h = ln2/0.833 d-1 = 0.832 d = 20.0 h. growth_method=calculated (mu=D from HRT, stated explicitly by authors). EPS 239.6 +/- 37.7 mg/L is a steady-state standing concentration (glucose-equivalent), not a volumetric rate. Iron consumption quantified as C_Fe,in - C_Fe,out by ICP-MS with EDTA-wash separating adsorbed vs internal fractions; absolute specific Fe uptake rate not tabulated in fulltext (Fig 3 / Table S5).

up.units: steady-state Fe consumption C_Fe,in - C_Fe,out; higher total q_Fe than at tau=1.2 d

sec.units: mg/L (steady-state standing concentration at tau=2.2 d)

growth_records.cite: Lucato, Veronica et al. Iron accumulation dynamics in Nostoc sp. PCC 7120: extent of biosorption in batch and continuous reactors. Biotechnology for Biofuels and Bioproducts (2026). DOI:10.1186/s13068-026-02743-9. [PMC12931063]
growth_records.snippet: at a residence time of 2.2 +/- 0.1 d, EPS levels increased to 110.0 +/- 13.0 mg... the growth rate... as the inverse of the residence time in continuous reactors
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote chemostat. mu = D = 1/tau; tau = 2.2 +/- 0.1 d => D = 1/2.2 = 0.4545 d-1 = 0.4545/24 = 0.0189 h-1; dilution_rate_per_h = growth_rate_per_h = 0.0189. doubling_time_h = ln2/0.4545 d-1 = 1.525 d = 36.6 h. growth_method=calculated (mu=D from HRT). EPS 110.0 +/- 13.0 mg/L is a standing steady-state concentration, not a volumetric rate. Longer HRT gave higher iron quota (lower dilution). Absolute specific Fe uptake not tabulated in fulltext.
